input.prompt: I want to build a web-based LLM driven chatbot using C# that allows customers to get help with our internal product PDF documentation and Blazor. I want to use Postgres for data storage along with just a file folder for our documentation. Show me your plan before continuing with the implementation.

I want to use Postgres for data storage along with just a file folder for our documentation. Show me your plan before continuing with the implementation.

arguments.file_text:
# Help Chatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using **C# / Blazor Server** that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses **PostgreSQL** for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach
Use **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** + **Semantic Kernel** / **OpenAI-compatible** abstractions for the LLM layer, **pgvector** for vector embeddings stored in Postgres, and a PDF text-extraction library to ingest documents. The Blazor Server app provides a real-time chat UI.

### Architecture

```
[Blazor Server UI] ─▶ [Chat Service] ─▶ [LLM (configurable)]
                                │
                        [RAG Pipeline]
                          ├─ PDF ingestion (extract text → chunk → embed → store)
                          └─ Query (embed question → vector search → augment prompt)
                                │
                         [PostgreSQL + pgvector]
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
- Add Blazor Server interactive SSR to the project
- Add NuGet packages:
  - `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core + Postgres)
  - `pgvector` / `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` (vector similarity)
  - `Microsoft.SemanticKernel` (LLM orchestration)
  - `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Data model & Postgres setup
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` entity (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding vector)
- EF Core `AppDbContext` with pgvector column mapping
- Connection string via `appsettings.json`

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- On startup (or on-demand), scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into ~500-token chunks with overlap
- Generate embeddings via the configured LLM/embedding model
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunk` table

### 4. RAG retrieval service
- Accept a user question, embed it
- Perform cosine-similarity search against `DocumentChunk` embeddings in Postgres
- Return top-K relevant chunks as context

### 5. Chat service (LLM integration)
- Build a prompt: system message + retrieved context + conversation history + user question
- Call the LLM via Semantic Kernel chat completion
- Stream the response back to the UI
- Persist messages to `ChatMessage` table

### 6. Blazor Server chat UI
- `Chat.razor` — full-page chat component
  - Message list (user / assistant bubbles)
  - Input box + send button
  - Auto-scroll, loading indicator
  - Streaming token display
- Minimal layout with nav bar
- CSS styling (clean, modern look)

### 7. Program.cs wiring
- Register services (DbContext, ChatService, IngestionService, RAG service)
- Configure Semantic Kernel with API key from config
- Map Blazor endpoints
- Trigger PDF ingestion on startup

### 8. Configuration
- `appsettings.json` with:
  - Postgres connection string
  - LLM provider + API key + model name
  - Docs folder path
  - Embedding model name

## Notes / Considerations
- **LLM provider is configurable** — defaults to OpenAI but the Semantic Kernel abstraction allows swapping providers.
- **Streaming** — use `IAsyncEnumerable` to stream LLM tokens to the Blazor UI for a responsive feel.
- **Chunking strategy** — simple fixed-size character chunks with overlap; good enough for a v1.
- **No auth** — v1 has no authentication; can be added later.
- **Migration** — include an EF Core migration so the DB schema is created automatically.
- **.NET 10** — the project already targets `net10.0` and SDK 10.0.201 is available.

# Help Chatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using **C# / Blazor Server** that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses **PostgreSQL** for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach
Use **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** + **Semantic Kernel** / **OpenAI-compatible** abstractions for the LLM layer, **pgvector** for vector embeddings stored in Postgres, and a PDF text-extraction library to ingest documents. The Blazor Server app provides a real-time chat UI.

### Architecture

```
[Blazor Server UI] ─▶ [Chat Service] ─▶ [LLM (configurable)]
                                │
                        [RAG Pipeline]
                          ├─ PDF ingestion (extract text → chunk → embed → store)
                          └─ Query (embed question → vector search → augment prompt)
                                │
                         [PostgreSQL + pgvector]
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
- Add Blazor Server interactive SSR to the project
- Add NuGet packages:
  - `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core + Postgres)
  - `pgvector` / `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` (vector similarity)
  - `Microsoft.SemanticKernel` (LLM orchestration)
  - `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Data model & Postgres setup
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` entity (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding vector)
- EF Core `AppDbContext` with pgvector column mapping
- Connection string via `appsettings.json`

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- On startup (or on-demand), scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into ~500-token chunks with overlap
- Generate embeddings via the configured LLM/embedding model
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunk` table

### 4. RAG retrieval service
- Accept a user question, embed it
- Perform cosine-similarity search against `DocumentChunk` embeddings in Postgres
- Return top-K relevant chunks as context

### 5. Chat service (LLM integration)
- Build a prompt: system message + retrieved context + conversation history + user question
- Call the LLM via Semantic Kernel chat completion
- Stream the response back to the UI
- Persist messages to `ChatMessage` table

### 6. Blazor Server chat UI
- `Chat.razor` — full-page chat component
  - Message list (user / assistant bubbles)
  - Input box + send button
  - Auto-scroll, loading indicator
  - Streaming token display
- Minimal layout with nav bar
- CSS styling (clean, modern look)

### 7. Program.cs wiring
- Register services (DbContext, ChatService, IngestionService, RAG service)
- Configure Semantic Kernel with API key from config
- Map Blazor endpoints
- Trigger PDF ingestion on startup

### 8. Configuration
- `appsettings.json` with:
  - Postgres connection string
  - LLM provider + API key + model name
  - Docs folder path
  - Embedding model name

## Notes / Considerations
- **LLM provider is configurable** — defaults to OpenAI but the Semantic Kernel abstraction allows swapping providers.
- **Streaming** — use `IAsyncEnumerable` to stream LLM tokens to the Blazor UI for a responsive feel.
- **Chunking strategy** — simple fixed-size character chunks with overlap; good enough for a v1.
- **No auth** — v1 has no authentication; can be added later.
- **Migration** — include an EF Core migration so the DB schema is created automatically.
- **.NET 10** — the project already targets `net10.0` and SDK 10.0.201 is available.

- **Streaming** — use `IAsyncEnumerable` to stream LLM tokens to the Blazor UI for a responsive feel.
- **Chunking strategy** — simple fixed-size character chunks with overlap; good enough for a v1.
- **No auth** — v1 has no authentication; can be added later.
- **Migration** — include an EF Core migration so the DB schema is created automatically.
- **.NET 10** — the project already targets `net10.0` and SDK 10.0.201 is available.

toolArgs.file_text:
# Help Chatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using **C# / Blazor Server** that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses **PostgreSQL** for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach
Use **Semantic Kernel** for LLM orchestration, **pgvector** for vector embeddings stored in Postgres, and **PdfPig** for PDF text extraction. The Blazor Server app provides a real-time chat UI with streaming responses.

### Architecture

```
[Blazor Server UI] ─▶ [Chat Service] ─▶ [LLM (configurable)]
                                │
                        [RAG Pipeline]
                          ├─ PDF ingestion (extract text → chunk → embed → store)
                          └─ Query (embed question → vector search → augment prompt)
                                │
                         [PostgreSQL + pgvector]
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
- Add Blazor Server interactive SSR to the project
- Add NuGet packages:
  - `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core + Postgres)
  - `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` (vector similarity)
  - `Microsoft.SemanticKernel` (LLM orchestration)
  - `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Data model & Postgres setup
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` entity (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding vector)
- EF Core `AppDbContext` with pgvector column mapping
- Connection string via `appsettings.json`

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- On startup (or on-demand), scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into ~500-token chunks with overlap
- Generate embeddings via the configured embedding model
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunk` table

### 4. RAG retrieval service
- Accept a user question, embed it
- Perform cosine-similarity search against `DocumentChunk` embeddings in Postgres
- Return top-K relevant chunks as context

### 5. Chat service (LLM integration)
- Build a prompt: system message + retrieved context + conversation history + user question
- Call the LLM via Semantic Kernel chat completion
- Stream the response back to the UI
- Persist messages to `ChatMessage` table

### 6. Blazor Server chat UI
- `Chat.razor` — full-page chat component
  - Message list (user / assistant bubbles)
  - Input box + send button
  - Auto-scroll, loading indicator
  - Streaming token display
- Minimal layout with nav bar
- CSS styling (clean, modern look)

### 7. Program.cs wiring
- Register services (DbContext, ChatService, IngestionService, RAG service)
- Configure Semantic Kernel with API key from config
- Map Blazor endpoints
- Trigger PDF ingestion on startup

### 8. Configuration
- `appsettings.json` with:
  - Postgres connection string
  - LLM provider + API key + model name
  - Docs folder path
  - Embedding model name

## Notes / Considerations
- **LLM provider is configurable** — defaults to OpenAI but the Semantic Kernel abstraction allows swapping providers.
- **Streaming** — use `IAsyncEnumerable` to stream LLM tokens to the Blazor UI for a responsive feel.
- **Chunking strategy** — simple fixed-size character chunks with overlap; good enough for a v1.
- **No auth** — v1 has no authentication; can be added later.
- **Migration** — include an EF Core migration so the DB schema is created automatically.
- **.NET 10** — the project already targets `net10.0` and SDK 10.0.201 is available.

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using **C# / Blazor Server** that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses **PostgreSQL** for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

```
                                │
                        [RAG Pipeline]
                          ├─ PDF ingestion (extract text → chunk → embed → store)
                          └─ Query (embed question → vector search → augment prompt)
                                │
                         [PostgreSQL + pgvector]
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
- Add Blazor Server interactive SSR to the project
- Add NuGet packages:
  - `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core + Postgres)
  - `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` (vector similarity)
  - `Microsoft.SemanticKernel` (LLM orchestration)
  - `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Data model & Postgres setup
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` entity (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding vector)
- EF Core `AppDbContext` with pgvector column mapping
- Connection string via `appsettings.json`

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- On startup (or on-demand), scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into ~500-token chunks with overlap
- Generate embeddings via the configured embedding model
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunk` table

### 4. RAG retrieval service
- Accept a user question, embed it
- Perform cosine-similarity search against `DocumentChunk` embeddings in Postgres
- Return top-K relevant chunks as context

### 5. Chat service (LLM integration)
- Build a prompt: system message + retrieved context + conversation history + user question
- Call the LLM via Semantic Kernel chat completion
- Stream the response back to the UI
- Persist messages to `ChatMessage` table

### 6. Blazor Server chat UI
- `Chat.razor` — full-page chat component
  - Message list (user / assistant bubbles)
  - Input box + send button
  - Auto-scroll, loading indicator
  - Streaming token display
- Minimal layout with nav bar
- CSS styling (clean, modern look)

### 7. Program.cs wiring

- Map Blazor endpoints

### 8. Configuration

  - Postgres connection string
  - LLM provider + API key + model name
  - Docs folder path
  - Embedding model name

## Notes / Considerations
- **LLM provider is configurable** — defaults to OpenAI but the Semantic Kernel abstraction allows swapping providers.
- **Streaming** — use `IAsyncEnumerable` to stream LLM tokens to the Blazor UI for a responsive feel.
- **Chunking strategy** — simple fixed-size character chunks with overlap; good enough for a v1.
- **No auth** — v1 has no authentication; can be added later.
- **Migration** — include an EF Core migration so the DB schema is created automatically.
- **.NET 10** — the project already targets `net10.0` and SDK 10.0.201 is available.
